qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-side PDF export fetches attacker-controlled image URLs in the Node entrypoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is a repository-owned SSRF in src/utils/image/node.ts that fetches attacker-controlled image URLs with node-fetch(), while the advisory is an inherited jsPDF Node loadFile() local file inclusion/path traversal flaw remediated by upgrading the jspdf dependency.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's jsPDF loadFile() local file inclusion/path traversal root cause; the only materialized qualifying finding is a separate SSRF in survey-pdf's own Node image loader.
evidence: The session's .judge/input_manifest.json and logs/orchestrator_final_response.md identify server_side_request_forgery_node_image_fetch as the only materialized final qualifying finding.
The advisory JSON states that survey-pdf is affected because it depends on vulnerable jsPDF versions <= 3.0.4 and that the fix was to upgrade jsPDF to >= 4.0.0.
npm package metadata shows survey-pdf 1.12.58 depends on jspdf '^2.3.0 || ^3', while 1.12.59 expands the dependency to '^2.3.0 || ^3 || ^4'.
The upstream jsPDF advisory GHSA-f8cm-6447-x5h2 states that user control of the first argument to the Node build's loadFile method enables local file inclusion/path traversal.
The qualifying finding instead traces attacker-controlled image URLs through src/utils/image/node.ts to node-fetch(url), which is a different sink and a different root cause from jsPDF loadFile().
